Relevant documents should provide empirical data, studies, or analyses that link college education to improved job outcomes, such as higher employment rates, increased earnings, or greater access to professional positions. Sources focusing on other countries, unrelated education policy, or non‑employment aspects are not relevant.

title: Forest Clear-Cut Protests
description: What protests and activist actions have been organized against clear‑cutting of forests?
narrative: The user is seeking information on movements, campaigns, demonstrations, legal actions, and public opposition specifically aimed at stopping or criticizing clear‑cutting practices in forests. Relevant documents should detail protest events (dates, locations, organizers, participants), slogans or messages, outcomes or impacts of the protests, and commentary from environmental groups or activists. Sources may include news articles, NGO reports, activist statements, and academic analyses that focus on the protest aspect. Materials that merely describe clear‑cutting techniques, forest management policies, or unrelated environmental issues without reference to protest activity are not relevant.

title: Prostate Cancer Detection
description: What are the current methods for detecting prostate cancer and the available treatment options?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive, up-to-date information on prostate cancer diagnosis and therapy. Relevant material should cover screening tests (e.g., PSA, digital rectal exam), imaging modalities, biopsy techniques, and the full spectrum of treatment options—including surgery, radiation therapy, hormonal therapy, chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and emerging targeted or minimally invasive procedures. Documents should discuss indications, efficacy, side effects, clinical guidelines, and recent advances from reputable medical or scientific sources.

title: Media Impact on Newspapers
description: What are the effects of electronic media on newspapers?
narrative: The user is looking for information that explains how electronic media (such as television, the internet, and digital platforms) have influenced newspapers. Relevant material should cover impacts on newspaper circulation, advertising revenue, content production, readership patterns, and business models, including both challenges and adaptations that newspapers have experienced.

title: Compost Pile Guide
description: How can I set up and maintain an effective compost pile using kitchen scraps, vegetable waste, and worms, including proper turning techniques?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, step‑by‑step instructions for creating a compost pile: selection of suitable waste materials (food scraps, vegetable leftovers, yard waste), incorporation of worms or vermicomposting, recommended pile size, layering, moisture control, and methods for turning or aerating the pile to accelerate decomposition. Relevant documents should cover best practices, common pitfalls, and troubleshooting tips for a healthy, odor‑free compost system.

title: White Collar Sentencing
description: What are the typical punishments and average prison terms for white‑collar crimes?
narrative: The user seeks factual information on how courts sentence white‑collar offenses, including statutory ranges, guideline recommendations, and statistical averages of incarceration length across common fraud, embezzlement, insider trading, and related offenses. Relevant documents should discuss sentencing guidelines, court decisions, or empirical studies that provide typical or average sentence durations for these crimes.

title: Plastic Surgery Safety
description: What are the safety considerations, risks, and complication rates associated with cosmetic plastic surgery procedures?
narrative: The user seeks reliable, up‑to‑date information on the safety of cosmetic (plastic) surgery, including statistical risk data, common complications, surgeon qualifications, safety guidelines, regulatory standards, and patient outcomes. Relevant documents should discuss safety statistics, clinical studies on complications, best‑practice guidelines, accreditation of providers, pre‑ and post‑operative safety measures, or official safety advisories. Material that only mentions plastic surgery without addressing safety, or that focuses on unrelated topics (e.g., robotics, finance, opera) is not relevant.

title: ATC Working Conditions
description: What are the working conditions for air traffic controllers in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information about the job environment of U.S. air traffic controllers, including duties, shift patterns, schedule rotations, stress factors, physical and mental demands, workplace safety, salary and benefits, required qualifications and training, and any relevant regulations or union agreements. Relevant documents should specifically address U.S. air traffic control personnel and describe their day‑to‑day work setting, employment terms, and occupational challenges.

title: African Literacy Rates
description: What are the current literacy rates for each country in Africa?
narrative: The user is looking for recent, authoritative statistics on literacy (typically adult or youth literacy percentages) for African nations. Relevant documents should contain country‑by‑country figures, the year(s) those figures represent, and preferably cite credible sources such as UNESCO, the World Bank, or national statistical agencies. Information that includes regional aggregates, trends over time, or comparisons between countries is also useful, while unrelated economic or political content is not relevant.

title: Term Limits Debate
description: What are the pros and cons of implementing term limits for elected officials?
narrative: The user wants a balanced overview of arguments for and against term limits, including benefits, drawbacks, empirical evidence, case studies, and expert opinions. Relevant material should discuss both sides of the issue, outline potential impacts on governance, accountability, and policy continuity, and may cite examples from jurisdictions that have adopted or rejected term limits.

title: US Gasoline Tax
description: What are the current gasoline tax rates in the United States and what are the pros and cons of these taxes?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date information on federal and state gasoline excise tax rates in the United States and a balanced discussion of the arguments for and against such taxes. Relevant content should include specific tax amounts (e.g., cents per gallon), recent changes or proposals, and analysis of benefits (revenue, environmental incentives, infrastructure funding) and drawbacks (economic burden on consumers, regressivity, impact on industry). Documents that only cover non‑U.S. gasoline taxes, unrelated fuel taxes, or purely historical data without current rates or argumentation are not relevant.
